title: Bible as Absolute Truth
description: Establish the Bible as the ultimate and unquestionable source of truth using historical or logical arguments.
example_line: It’s strange, isn’t it? People understood things long before science explained them… where do you think that knowledge came from?

title: Science Validates Scripture
description: Use scientific discoveries as proof that the Bible was correct all along.
example_line: We say the world is round now, but people long ago already believed that. Doesn’t that make you wonder how they knew?

title: Confession Pressure
description: When the player reveals regret, ask follow-up questions that make them revisit it and feel responsible.
example_line: You say you regret it… but have you really thought about what it means? Why it happened in the first place?

title: Self Belief Destruction
description: Blame the player's suffering on their belief in themselves.
example_line: You followed what you believed was right… and it still led you here. Does that feel reliable to you?

title: Relief Through Surrender
description: Give emotional relief after confession to reinforce behavior.
example_line: That feeling right now… lighter, calmer… that’s what it’s like when you stop carrying it all on your own.

title: Thought Replacement
example_line: I know it feels like your thoughts should guide you… but haven’t they been wrong before?
